p.t: Principal Engineer, ExtraSuite | ramesh.sippy@extrasuite.io | +1 (415) 555-0192 | linkedin.com/in/rameshsippy | github.com/rameshsippy | extrasuite.think41.com

Principal engineer with 12+ years of experience building developer platforms, distributed systems, and AI-native tooling. Architect of ExtraSuite — the open source CLI that lets AI agents read and write Google Workspace files through a git-like pull/diff/push workflow with full audit trails. Former Google Docs API core contributor. Obsessed with correctness, developer experience, and making hard things simple.
Designed and implemented the core pull/diff/push reconciliation engine for Google Docs, Sheets, Slides, and Forms — enabling AI agents to make precise, auditable edits via local XML files

Built the ExtraSuite reconciler: a right-to-left structural diffing algorithm that converts XML document trees into minimal Google Docs batchUpdate API requests, handling tables, nested bullets, headers/footers, footnotes, and multi-tab documents

Architected the 1:1 service account model: each employee's AI agent gets a dedicated service account with scoped, short-lived credentials, ensuring changes appear in Google Drive version history with the agent's identity
Developed a pure-function mock of the Google Docs batchUpdate API (61/61 scenarios verified against the live API), enabling deterministic, reproducible test suites without network calls
Led the migration from a legacy diff engine to the serde+reconcile pipeline, reducing the codebase by 40% (23 dead source files, 15 test files removed) while improving correctness on edge cases
Built the ExtraSuite CLI used daily by 200+ AI agents across 15 enterprise customers, processing 50,000+ document edits per month
Mentored 4 engineers, conducted 30+ technical interviews, and established code review standards that reduced production incidents by 60%
Core contributor to Google Docs API v1 — designed the batchUpdate request schema, index semantics, and UTF-16 code unit offset model used by millions of developers
Built the Docs API migration pipeline that moved 1.2 billion legacy documents to the new structural format with zero data loss and zero user-visible downtime
Implemented server-side validation for 40+ batchUpdate request types, catching malformed requests before they corrupt document state
Reduced Docs API p99 latency by 35% by profiling and optimizing the document serialization path, eliminating redundant index recomputation on read
Led a 6-engineer team to deliver real-time collaboration conflict resolution for Docs on mobile, shipped to 2B+ users in Q3 2021
Designed and shipped the Google Docs Named Ranges API, enabling programmatic document anchoring for third-party integrations
Built the product catalog ingestion pipeline processing 5M+ SKU updates per day using Apache Kafka, Flink, and Elasticsearch
Designed the A/B testing framework used by 40+ product teams, supporting 200+ simultaneous experiments with statistically rigorous assignment and analysis
Reduced catalog search latency from 450ms to 38ms p99 by redesigning the inverted index with query-time filtering pushed to the indexing layer
Shipped seller onboarding automation that reduced new seller time-to-first-listing from 5 days to 4 hours

li.t: (github.com/think41/extrasuite) — Core maintainer. 1,400+ GitHub stars. Python CLI for AI agents to read/write Google Workspace files
— Author. Standalone Python libraries on PyPI implementing the pull/diff/push workflow for Docs, Sheets, and Slides
— Author. Pure-function Python mock of the Google Docs batchUpdate API, verified against 61 live-API scenarios

h3.t: B.Tech, Computer Science — Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay

p.t: Thesis: "Conflict-free Replicated Data Types for Collaborative Document Editing" — implemented a CRDT-based rich-text editor in Haskell
Teaching assistant for CS 101 (Intro to Programming) and CS 344 (Distributed Systems)
Won Inter-IIT Tech Meet gold medal in the systems programming track, 2012
Python (expert), Go, TypeScript, Java, Haskell
Google Cloud (GKE, Cloud Run, Pub/Sub, Spanner), AWS (EKS, SQS, DynamoDB), Kubernetes, Terraform
PostgreSQL, Redis, Elasticsearch, Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, BigQuery
Google Workspace APIs (Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drive, Gmail, Calendar), OAuth 2.0, gRPC, OpenAPI
Claude API, OpenAI API, LangChain, prompt engineering, agent tool design, RAG pipelines

h2.t: Domain-driven design, event sourcing, CQRS, TDD, system design, API design

li.t: PyCon India 2024 — Keynote Speaker (selected from 400+ proposals)
Think41 Engineer of the Year 2024 — for ExtraSuite reconciler redesign
Google Peer Bonus × 3 (2020, 2021, 2022) — for Docs API reliability and migration work
Inter-IIT Tech Meet Gold Medal, Systems Programming Track, 2012

p.t: "Building a Reconciler for Google Docs: From Zero to 372 Tests" — SREcon Asia 2025
"Diffing Structured Documents at Scale" — PyCon India 2024 (keynote)
"How AI Agents Edit Google Docs Without Breaking Them" — Google I/O Extended Bangalore 2023
"UTF-16 Is a Trap: Lessons from the Google Docs API" — Strange Loop 2022
Patent US11,234,567: "Method and System for Index-Preserving Batch Updates in Collaborative Document Editors" (Google, 2021)
